# AMO-Lean vs Plonky3: C Benchmark Report (v2 — post-bugfix)

**Date**: 2026-03-22
**Platform**: Apple Silicon (ARM64), macOS Darwin 24.6.0
**Compiler**: Apple clang, `-O2`
**Comparison**: AMO-Lean (e-graph + cost-model optimized) vs Plonky3 (Montgomery REDC)
**Note**: Post-bugfix run. Generated by `lake env lean --run Bench.lean -- --lang c --field all --size 14,18,22 --primitive ntt`

## Raw Data (NTT only — the primitive where strategies differ)

| field | log_n | n | primitive | amo_us | p3_us | diff_pct | amo_strategy | p3_strategy |
|-------|-------|---|-----------|--------|-------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| BabyBear | 14 | 16384 | NTT | 217.8 | 207.5 | -4.9 | Solinas | Montgomery |
| BabyBear | 18 | 262144 | NTT | 3664.4 | 4294.7 | +14.7 | Solinas | Montgomery |
| BabyBear | 22 | 4194304 | NTT | 67308.2 | 84655.8 | +20.5 | Solinas | Montgomery |
| KoalaBear | 14 | 16384 | NTT | 208.7 | 207.3 | -0.7 | Solinas | Montgomery |
| KoalaBear | 18 | 262144 | NTT | 3788.8 | 4294.8 | +11.8 | Solinas | Montgomery |
| KoalaBear | 22 | 4194304 | NTT | 70496.0 | 87133.2 | +19.1 | Solinas | Montgomery |
| Mersenne31 | 14 | 16384 | NTT | 169.8 | 213.5 | +20.5 | Solinas | Montgomery |
| Mersenne31 | 18 | 262144 | NTT | 3673.1 | 4403.8 | +16.6 | Solinas | Montgomery |
| Mersenne31 | 22 | 4194304 | NTT | 68236.6 | 86896.8 | +21.5 | Solinas | Montgomery |

## Summary

| Field | N=2^14 | N=2^18 | N=2^22 |
|-------|--------|--------|--------|
| **BabyBear** | -4.9% | +14.7% | +20.5% |
| **KoalaBear** | -0.7% | +11.8% | +19.1% |
| **Mersenne31** | +20.5% | +16.6% | +21.5% |

## Notes

- At small N (2^14), BabyBear and KoalaBear show no advantage or slight regression. The corrected butterfly has more overhead at small sizes where setup cost dominates.
- At production sizes (N >= 2^18), consistent +12-21% advantage across all 31-bit fields.
- Mersenne31 is the most consistent (+17-21%) because c=1 makes the fold trivial: `(x >> 31) + (x & mask)`.
- For Poly eval, FRI fold, and Dot product: both sides use Montgomery+Harvey — difference is ~0% (not shown here, see amo-lean-bench-c.md for full data).

## Changes from v1 (pre-bugfix, March 20)

- BabyBear 2^14: was +20.6%, now -4.9% (corrected butterfly has more overhead at small N)
- BabyBear 2^22: was +13.2%, now +20.5% (corrected code is actually faster at large N)
- KoalaBear 2^14: was +20.8%, now -0.7% (same small-N effect)
- Mersenne31: minimal change (its fold was already correct — c=1, no truncation issues)
